threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'sandwich' when you order from the local shops. It's a 'bacon sarnie' here, mate. Little things matter!
Last night on the 35, a couple argued over a missing shoe. Turns out, it was under the seat the whole time. Classic Brixton chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid in a schnitzel costume riding a skateboard past a café while their parents sipped kombucha. It's a unique blend here.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a laundromat that doubles as a bar. Sip a Kölsch while waiting for your clothes—only in Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to avoid BART Civic Center on weekends—loud bangs and smoke are just part of the charm, but the evacs are a hassle.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today where a guy was negotiating the fare with a bag of peanuts. Only in the TL could that be normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just the Gaudí sights, but the real charm is the hidden plazas where locals sip vermut and chat for hours.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their oversized backpacks and the way they hesitate at every metro entrance, like it's a labyrinth.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for a 'plastic bag' at the market instead of just saying 'bag'. The vendors give you that look like you’ve just landed from Mars.
Last weekend, saw a guy in a full tux singing opera to the night bus passengers. His voice was surprisingly good but the bus driver looked furious.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler chasing pigeons while their dad argued with the kebab shop owner over extra spicy sauce. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you see a techno party start at noon on a Tuesday. Who needs sleep when you have Berghain vibes at brunch?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you can spot a tourist by the way they flinch at the sirens. We just shrug and keep walking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched two guys in suits debating Muni’s delays while a pigeon casually helped itself to a dropped burrito in the middle of the street.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just tapas bars—they miss the local shortcuts through the park that lead to the real heart of the neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewildered expressions when the trams are late; locals just shrug and check their phones.
